title: Cross-project Obsidian plugin plan of record
objective: Reconcile every existing Lappe Linter and Product Management plan into one coordination contract with separate repository-owned execution lanes, accurate statuses, and no duplicated linter or pipeline ownership.

source.notes: All six plan documents were read in full. The two Lappe plans and the earlier Lappe consolidated plan are coordinated here; the three Product Management plans remain repository-local source records but their statuses and ownership are rolled up here. The Lappe backlog is empty and the Product Management backlog contains only .gitkeep.

problem.statement: The repositories have multiple plans with overlapping language but different scopes and inconsistent completion states. The Lappe settings plan is marked DONE while explicitly recording partial or incomplete work; the Product Management pipeline research plan remains DRAFT even though an implementation plan supersedes it and has already landed part of the work; the active product-intent pointer is separate and its relationship to the cross-project work is implicit.
evidence: lappe-linter/docs/plans/2026-07-10-settings-redesign-plan.md is marked DONE while preview is PARTIAL and scope autocomplete remains a follow-up.
lappe-linter/docs/plans/2026-07-10-settings-tabs-redesign-plan.md identifies the real settings-shell defect and remains DRAFT.
lappe-linter/docs/plans/2026-07-12-lappe-linter-consolidated-delivery-plan.md is itself a draft coordination plan and does not include the full Product Management workstream roll-up.

product-management-plugin/docs/plans/2026-07-04-pipeline-integration-implementation-plan.md states that it supersedes the research plan, while ACTIVE-PLAN.pointer still points to the product-intent plan.
The Product Management pipeline implementation plan records the consumption core as landed but leaves producer, render-surface, config-path, and optional capture work open.
Neither repository backlog contains actionable items that supersede the plan documents.

goal.statement: An agent can select one repository-owned lane, see its true status, understand its dependency boundary, execute its checks, and know which work is complete, in progress, new, superseded, or parked without merging the two plugins.
goal.baseline: Six plan documents span four workstreams with contradictory or implicit authority and no single cross-project status map.
goal.target: One validated coordination plan maps every source plan to one status and one owner lane, with no cyclic dependencies or duplicated implementation ownership.

non_goals: Merge the two repositories or make either plugin depend on the other at source level.
Delete historical plans, backlog directories, or user-owned dirty work.
Re-implement the already-tested Lappe core or Product Management consumption core.
Treat a plan status as proof that the corresponding Obsidian behavior is shipped.
Resolve the external session-data producer or capture decisions without the required local data evidence.
assumptions: lappe-linter owns Markdown lint execution, linter.yaml, formatting rules, scopes, and Obsidian lint commands.
product-management-plugin owns product intent, product notes, dashboards, indexes, and read-only cost consumption.
The Product Management ACTIVE-PLAN.pointer remains a repository-local pointer; this file is the cross-project coordination plan.
The existing Lappe and Product Management pure-core tests are reusable and do not need to be replaced by this reconciliation.
Any external producer lane begins only after its repository state, local data availability, and export contract are verified.
decisions_needed: DEC-001: Confirm this file is the cross-project coordination plan while each repository may retain a local plan pointer for its own implementation lane.
DEC-002: For Lappe, decide whether linter-styles are intentionally Obsidian-only or must reach CLI parity.
DEC-003: For Lappe, choose the final non-blocking preview workflow and complete scope autocomplete before marking the settings lane done.
DEC-004: For Product Management pipeline, choose the final render surface and exact cost-index path without changing the read-only contract.
DEC-005: For session-data, verify real token availability and choose the producer export path before scheduling the external producer lane.
DEC-006: Keep optional artifact-id capture gated on the existing D-049 decision; do not fabricate attribution.
risks: Marking the old Lappe DONE plan complete would cause agents to skip the actual settings-shell work.
The Lappe current UI refactor contains a constructor mismatch and undefined unreachable reference that esbuild does not catch.
Product pipeline work can drift if the research plan, implementation plan, and active pointer are treated as independent authorities.
A shared Obsidian vault or copied source can make the two plugins appear integrated while their configuration and rule engines remain separate.
The external session-data producer may be blocked by missing local reports or null token evidence.
Manual Obsidian verification remains necessary because repository tests do not prove settings registration, reload, or active-vault artifact selection.

requirements.statement: Every source plan is mapped to an observed status, rolled-up status, owner, and disposition in this coordination plan.
The plan explicitly separates Lappe linting ownership, Product Management product-intent ownership, and session-data telemetry ownership.
Lappe settings work is treated as INPRG: the real tabs, scope autocomplete, preview workflow, type-aware gate, and Obsidian verification remain open until evidenced.
Lappe runtime work preserves the existing core and closes wand, reload, CLI-boundary, artifact-install, and manual-vault verification gaps.
The Product Management product-intent plan remains INPRG and remains the local plan-of-record for its product hierarchy, artifact, CLI, and view requirements.
The Product Management pipeline research plan is treated as superseded input, while its unresolved questions are carried into the implementation lane rather than lost.
The Product Management pipeline consumption core remains INPRG with its frozen product-cost-index contract, null-safe attribution, and open producer/render/config tasks clearly separated.
The session-data producer lane is parked until local data evidence, export path, and attribution grain are confirmed; no fabricated telemetry is introduced.
The Product Management plugin does not become a second Lappe-compatible linter; any future rule integration uses an explicit provider or versioned contract.
A lane is not marked complete until its repository checks and required human Obsidian validation have recorded evidence from the exact artifact under test.

tests.expected: The coordination plan validates with no schema errors and all IDs, requirements, tests, and dependencies resolve.
The ownership boundary is explicit: the Lappe settings plan has no pipeline dependency and the pipeline research plan has no Lappe implementation dependency.
tests.evidence: Cross-plan negative reference scan plus status roll-up review.

tests.command: npm test && npm run build && npm run minify-css
tests.expected: Lappe tests, production build, and CSS generation pass for the settings/runtime lane.
tests.evidence: Record suite count, build output, and artifact hash in the Lappe passoff.

tests.command: npm test && npm run build && npm run check-versions
tests.expected: Product Management tests, build, and version checks pass for the product-intent and pipeline consumer lanes.
tests.evidence: Record command output in the Product Management passoff.

tests.expected: The frozen product-cost-index contract remains null-safe and the existing preview/consumer logic remains tested without Lappe source imports.
tests.evidence: Targeted test output and dependency scan.

tests.expected: Obsidian loads the exact Lappe artifact; the requested tabs, wand, example note, scopes, persistence, and reload behavior work in the throwaway vault.
tests.evidence: Record vault path, artifact SHA-256, tab names, lint result, YAML diff, scope result, and reload result.

tests.command: npm test && npm run build && npm run package
tests.expected: The Product Management plugin remains independently shippable and its cost view consumes only the documented read-only export contract.
tests.evidence: Record tests, build/package output, and a fixture or vault readback showing null-safe cost rendering.

tests.expected: The session-data repository, active branch, local report availability, token-availability evidence, and intended product-cost-index output path are identified before the producer lane is unparked.
tests.evidence: Record exact blocker or evidence; do not infer missing telemetry.

prs.title: docs: establish one cross-project plan of record
prs.objective: Make this coordination plan authoritative for cross-project status and preserve the repository-local plans as scoped historical or execution inputs.
tasks: Retain all source plans as evidence and mark the old Lappe DONE plan as superseded or historical when the docs lane is implemented.
Use this file as the cross-project status map without changing repository ownership or deleting local pointers.
Record that the Lappe backlog is empty and Product Management's backlog contains only .gitkeep.
Keep the Lappe/product boundary explicit: ordinary Markdown may be linted, but neither plugin imports the other's source.

preflight: Read the newest passoff for each repository.
Confirm both primary checkouts remain browse-only.
negative: Confirm no product source paths are added to the Lappe plugin build.

acceptance: Every plan artifact appears in the status roll-up.
No plan is silently deleted or falsely marked complete.
The two plugin ownership boundaries are explicit.
The plan validates as an agent-executable contract.
prs.rollback: Revert only the coordination-plan documentation; preserve all source and historical plan files.

prs.title: docs: reconcile Product Management plan authorities
prs.objective: Align the local Product Management plan statuses and references with the cross-project coordination plan without changing plugin behavior.
tasks: Keep the product-intent stack plan as the local active plan-of-record and preserve its INPRG scope.
Mark the pipeline research plan as superseded input or archive it with a direct pointer to the implementation plan; preserve unresolved questions in the implementation plan or this coordination plan.
Keep the pipeline implementation plan INPRG with its landed consumption core and explicit remaining producer/render/config/capture work.
Add a concise boundary note that Lappe Linter is not a pipeline dependency.

preflight: Confirm origin/main is current and inspect dirty files before editing.
ci: Product Management build, test, version, and documentation checks.
negative: Confirm ACTIVE-PLAN.pointer still resolves to the product-intent plan and no source behavior changes are included.

acceptance: Research and implementation plans no longer compete as equal active plans.
The product-intent plan remains INPRG rather than being incorrectly closed.
No plugin code changes are bundled.
prs.rollback: Revert only plan-status documentation; preserve the implementation and product-intent source plans.

prs.title: feat: finish Lappe settings and runtime delivery
prs.objective: Complete the Lappe settings architecture and runtime verification using the existing tested core without shipping the current unfinished UI refactor.
tasks: Start from a clean task worktree and preserve the current dirty checkout as user-owned evidence.
Wire the existing renderers into exactly General, YAML, Headers, Body, Special formatting, Scopes, Rule order, Custom, and Debug; remove Lappe and combined Style registration.
Fix the current LappeTab constructor mismatch and undefined unreachable code.
Restore vault-aware YAML and scope autocomplete/multi-select controls and complete the chosen non-blocking preview workflow.
Verify wand fallback, config reload, style-file/CLI boundaries, artifact installation, and the full Obsidian acceptance matrix.

preflight: Review current lappe-tab.ts diff before editing.
Confirm no dirty primary files are staged.
local: npx tsc --noEmit with documented baseline comparison
ci: Full Lappe test suite, build, CSS generation, and package checks.
negative: No Lappe or Style settings tab.
No silent wand no-op for reading view or no active file.

acceptance: Requested settings tabs are visible in Obsidian.
Core behavior, YAML persistence, scope resolution, inheritance, and rule order remain intact.
The current type/API defects are removed.
The exact installed artifact passes manual Obsidian verification.
prs.rollback: Revert the isolated Lappe delivery branch only; do not reset the dirty primary checkout or remove vault data.

prs.title: feat: complete Product Management pipeline consumption
prs.objective: Finish only the Product Management side of the frozen product-cost-index contract after the remaining render-surface and path decisions are resolved.
tasks: Do not redo the landed parser, attribution, null-safety, or cycle-guard core.
Resolve the exact export drop path and add additive config wiring.
Choose and implement one render surface for the read-only dataset.
Keep session-data pricing authoritative and preserve unknown/null display behavior.
Leave optional exact capture gated on the external D-049 decision.

preflight: Confirm the session-data contract and origin/main are current.
integration: TEST-007 after a real product-cost-index fixture is produced.
negative: No telemetry writes into authored product notes.
Null cost/token/CO2 values never render as zero.
Ambiguous joins are excluded from summed cost.

acceptance: The plugin reads the documented export without owning production of telemetry.
One dashboard or view renders the contract with null-safe semantics.
The product-intent plan remains independent of pipeline implementation details.
prs.rollback: No implementation starts until DEC-004 and the producer contract are resolved.

prs.title: feat: produce product-cost-index from session-data
prs.objective: Produce the versioned product-cost-index export from real session-data evidence without fabricating missing tokens, cost, CO2, or attribution.
tasks: Inventory the active schema, local reports, token availability, attribution grain, and exact vault export path.
Choose the join key and preserve the frozen product-cost-index contract.
Emit a deterministic export with null-safe fields and no writes into authored product notes.
Add producer fixtures and negative tests for missing evidence, ambiguous joins, and unknown pricing.

preflight: Confirm local session-data reports are available and current.
local: Producer unit tests and schema validation.
integration: TEST-007 after the export is consumed by Product Management.
negative: No runtime network pull or untracked source is silently treated as evidence.

acceptance: The producer is unblocked by real local evidence.
The export is versioned, deterministic, and contract-valid.
The Product Management consumer can read it without mutating authored notes.
prs.rollback: Keep the lane parked and retain research evidence if local reports or attribution inputs are unavailable.

preflight: Read the newest passoff for each repository before selecting a lane.
Confirm the target repo, default branch, and task worktree.
Run git status --short --branch and preserve dirty user-owned files.
scheduling: Run PR-001 before any implementation lane.
Run PR-002 for Product Management plan authority and PR-003 for Lappe delivery independently after PR-001.
Do not unpark PR-004 until its render/path decisions are resolved.
Do not unpark PR-005 until TEST-008 supplies local evidence.
closeout: Record exact artifact/vault evidence for Obsidian validation.
Update the relevant passoff and this coordination plan's workstream status without marking partial work done.

features.statement: Cross-project plan authority and ownership boundary
Canonical Lappe settings and runtime delivery

validations.statement: Product Management repository and consumer validation

status_rollup.disposition: Historical implementation log; not complete because it records partial preview, free-text scope controls, and the old settings architecture.
Primary source for the Lappe settings requirements; its tasks are carried into PR-003.
This is now the cross-project coordination plan; its Lappe PR lanes are retained in PR-003.
Remains Product Management's local plan-of-record; no Lappe dependency.
Research findings are carried into the implementation plan and PR-004/PR-005 decisions; unresolved questions remain visible.
Consumption core is landed; producer, render surface, config path, and optional capture remain open.
No backlog item was promoted into this plan without evidence.

metadata.authority: This plan coordinates cross-project sequencing. Repository-local pointers remain valid for local execution, but no agent should treat a superseded source plan as a second active implementation lane.
